concept_rationale.purpose: To create a physical interface (drill jig/alignment tool) for an existing digital part.
concept_rationale.logic: The template acts as a 'negative' or 'stencil' of the reference part's mounting features.
concept_rationale.hardcoding_intent: Template dimensions (width, height, thickness) are independent of the source part's dimensions to allow for material saving or specific jig-clamping requirements without polluting the source project's logic.
concept_rationale.integrity: The reference model must remain a 'read-only' visual guide to guarantee that the final 3D printed template matches the real-world hardware perfectly.
concept_rationale.alignment_register: Use an L-shaped base (lip/register) that hooks onto the physical part for perfect, reproducible alignment.

code_style_constraints.comments_rule: NO comments or citations allowed inside the code body. Comments are ONLY permitted at the top of the parameters section.

workflow_logic.parameter_separation: All template-specific sizing must be HARDCODED as local variables at the top. Do NOT import these from parameters.scad.

linking.include: Used for shared constants (hole spacing, diameters) to ensure 1:1 synchronization.
linking.use: Used for reference geometry to prevent unwanted ghost-objects in the final STL.

header: tpl_clearance = 0.2; // Extra room for drill bit/bolt fit
core_structure.debug_mode: Include a 'show_reference = true;' toggle for alignment verification.

geometry_logic.base: The 'substrate' or plate of the template, driven by tpl_ variables. Combine with an alignment lip (using union) based on original part dimensions.

subtraction.epsilon_offset: Add 0.01mm (z-diff) to subtraction cylinders to ensure clean manifold cuts.
subtraction.feature_mapping: Subtract holes/slots using positions defined in parameters.scad, but applying local tpl_clearance.
subtraction.extended_reach: Make subtraction cylinders extra long (e.g., h=50) to guarantee they cut entirely through the template even when offset.

ai_system_tips.naming_convention: Prefix all local variables with 'tpl_' to distinguish them from project parameters.
ai_system_tips.z_fighting_fix: Always use 'translate([0, 0, -1]) cylinder(h = tpl_thickness + 2, ...)' for holes.
ai_system_tips.origin_alignment: Ask the user if the reference model is centered (center=true) before calculating hole offsets.
ai_system_tips.flush_mounting: Translate the template by half the reference part thickness plus half the template thickness to place it perfectly flush against the surface.

common_pitfalls.parameter_drift: Avoid redefining hole distances locally; always pull from the include file.
common_pitfalls.fitment_issues: Ensure tpl_clearance is added to the diameter, otherwise the drill bit will bind.
